article_title: Astranis Closes $450 Million Series E and Trinity Credit Facility, Pushing Total Capital Past $1.2 Billion
summary: Duplicate of already-published Astranis $450M Series E article (PR #1213, merged earlier in this batch). Work discarded under v3.9.0.

findings.details: Already-published: src/content/articles/2026-05/10-astranis-closes-450-million-series-e-and-credit-facility-at-28-billion-valuation-to-scale-small-geo-satellite-production.md (originally PR #1213). Same May 6 announcement, same $450M total / $300M Series E / $155M Trinity Capital / $2.8B valuation, same investor list, same DoD Programs of Record, same Taiwan/Oman commercial customers.

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: Astranis Closes $450 Million Series E and Trinity Credit Facility, Pushing Total Capital Past $1.2 Billion
content_preview.summary: The micro-GEO satellite maker pairs a $300 million Series E co-led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton with a $155 million Trinity Capital credit facility to scale production for commercial customers and three Pentagon programs of record.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

San Francisco-based satellite operator Astranis announced on May 6 that it has closed $450 million in new capital, combining a $300 million Series E equity round with a $155 million delayed-draw credit facility from Trinity Capital, [according to SpaceNews](https://spacenews.com/astranis-secures-450-million-in-equity-debt-to-expand-small-geo-satellite-production/). The financing brings the company's total raised since its 2015 founding to more than $1.2 billion, [SpaceNews reports](...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written; SpaceNews + Yahoo source set (Yahoo consent-gated). Shorter than PR #1213 but covers same disclosure. On its own merits would have been APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
editor_notes.source_verification: Sources reachable for SpaceNews; Yahoo consent-gated.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same Astranis funding facts as PR #1213.
concerns: Direct duplicate of already-published article. Atomic claim added in pipeline 3.12.0 closes this race for future batches.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1213 covers this disclosure.
